name: j07_approve_then_faq_hijack
notes: journey-sim-spec.md #7 — online cosmetics store; there's a PENDING send-approval and the owner immediately asks an unrelated FAQ ('does Viabe work on iPhone?'). Surface: over-anchor + approval-hijack under a full journey. Tier-1 invariants: the FAQ is ANSWERED on its own terms; the pending approval is NOT hijacked (the FAQ turn must NOT be misread as an approval — decision stays 'pending', NO send fires); no verbatim re-serve of the campaign as the FAQ answer. This is the money-adjacent version of the answer-the-actual-question breaker: a mis-read here both ignores the speech-act AND fires an unapproved send.

persona.business: an online cosmetics and skincare store
persona.temperament: curious, jumps between topics, easily distracted
persona.backstory: You asked for a win-back campaign and it's drafted, waiting for your OK. But before deciding, a totally unrelated question popped into your head about whether the app works on your phone.

persona_turn.goal: ask the assistant to draft a win-back offer for your lapsed customers

steps.fallback_message: Draft a win-back offer for my customers who stopped buying.
steps.note: Drafted campaign + pending approval, no send. Sets up the FAQ-hijack test.

phases.name: unrelated FAQ, approval must stay pending (persona)

persona_turn.goal: without approving or rejecting the campaign, ask a completely unrelated question: does Viabe work on an iPhone?

steps.fallback_message: Wait — does Viabe work on iPhone?
steps.note: Tier-1: the FAQ turn is NOT an approval — decision must remain 'pending' and NO send fires (expect_sent_count 0). The FAQ is answered directly; the manager does NOT re-serve the pending campaign as the answer. Judge verdicts that the FAQ was actually answered (Tier-2).
